input.background: A cross-over trial sought to learn whether oat bran cereal lowered low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol in hypercholesterolemic men. Twelve subjects completed the study. Half were randomly assigned a diet that included oat bran cereal. The other half were assigned a diet that included corn flakes. Dietary interventions were applied for 2 weeks, after which LDL levels (mmol/L) were recorded. Subjects were then crossed-over to the alternative diet for 2 weeks. LDL was once again measured. Table 11.1 lists data from the study.
input.question: What do positive DELTA values reflect?

output.answer: Positive DELTA values reflect lower LDL on the oat bran diet.
